nodes.label: Graphify Knowledge Graph Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Turns any folder of code/docs/papers/images/video into a persistent, queryable knowledge graph with an honest EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS audit trail; community detection surfaces cross-document connections the user would not think to ask about.

nodes.label: Structural AST Extraction
nodes.rationale: Deterministic and free: code is extracted structurally with no LLM and no API key, so a code-only corpus needs no semantic pass at all.

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction via Parallel Subagents
nodes.rationale: Docs/papers/images need LLM judgment; parallel general-purpose subagents (or Gemini if a key is set) are 5-10x faster than reading files one-by-one in the main session.

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction Cache
nodes.rationale: Checked before dispatching subagents so unchanged files never pay LLM cost twice; stale cache sidecars are deleted rather than merged (#1392).

nodes.label: Graph Health Check
nodes.rationale: Read-only integrity gate before labeling: surfaces edge collapse, dangling/missing endpoints, and self-loops — the silent-corruption modes of incremental updates and AST/LLM id mismatches. Never aborts, but warnings must be surfaced.

nodes.label: Graphify Honesty Rules
nodes.rationale: Never invent an edge, never hide cohesion scores, always show token cost — the audit trail is only useful if it is honest.

nodes.label: Query Fast Path
nodes.rationale: When graphify-out/graph.json already exists and the request is a natural-language question, skip the whole build pipeline and query immediately — the graph is already built.

nodes.label: Graph Shrink Guard (#479)
nodes.rationale: to_json refuses to write a graph smaller than the existing graph.json so an empty or partial extraction can never clobber a good graph; intentional shrinks require --force.

nodes.label: Deterministic Node ID Format
nodes.rationale: Full repo-relative path stem (every directory level) keeps same-named files distinct and must match the AST extractor's IDs — otherwise orphan ghost-duplicate nodes accumulate.

nodes.label: Discrete Confidence Rubric
nodes.rationale: Models follow discrete rubrics better than continuous ranges; production showed a bimodal collapse (>50% at 0.5), so INFERRED edges must pick one of five discrete values and never default to 0.5.

nodes.label: Hyperedges
nodes.rationale: Capture group participation (3+ nodes in one flow or pattern) that pairwise edges alone cannot express; used sparingly, max 3 per chunk.

nodes.label: Semantic Similarity Edges
nodes.rationale: Connect concepts that solve the same problem without any structural link; only for genuinely non-obvious, cross-cutting similarity.

nodes.label: Constrained Query Vocabulary Expansion
nodes.rationale: The query CLI matches by case-folded substring + IDF with no stemming or synonyms, so questions are expanded against the graph's own vocabulary file — tokens may only be picked from the actual vocab, never invented, keeping the expansion auditable.

nodes.label: BFS/DFS Graph Traversal
nodes.rationale: BFS for broad 'what connects to X' context, DFS for tracing a specific chain; answers must use only what the graph contains, citing source_location.

nodes.label: Work Memory save-result Feedback Loop
nodes.rationale: Every answered query is saved back into the graph with an outcome (useful/dead_end/corrected) so future sessions start from preferred sources and skip known dead ends via graphify reflect / LESSONS.md — a self-improving loop.

nodes.label: Incremental Update (--update)
nodes.rationale: Re-extracts only new/changed files to save tokens and time; code-only changes skip semantic extraction entirely.

nodes.label: build_merge Replace-on-Re-extract
nodes.rationale: Reads graph.json directly (no NetworkX round-trip) so edge direction survives (#801); prune_sources is only for genuinely deleted files because changed files are reconciled by replace-on-re-extract (#1344), and root= keeps prune paths on the same relative base (#1361).

nodes.label: Cluster-Only Rebuild
nodes.rationale: Self-contained re-cluster of the existing graph; Steps 5-9 must not re-run because their intermediate files were deleted by a prior build's cleanup (#1392).

nodes.label: URL Ingestion (/graphify add)
nodes.rationale: Auto-detects URL type (YouTube, Twitter/X, arXiv, PDF, image, webpage), saves to ./raw, then runs the --update pipeline to merge the new file into the graph.

nodes.label: Watch Mode Auto-Rebuild
nodes.rationale: Debounced folder watcher: code changes rebuild via AST with no LLM; doc/paper/image changes only flag needs_update because they require LLM re-extraction.

nodes.label: FalkorDB Export
nodes.rationale: FalkorDB's GRAPH.QUERY runs one statement at a time (no bulk script import), so direct --falkordb-push is preferred over the cypher.txt artifact.

nodes.label: Graph MCP Server
nodes.rationale: Stdio MCP server exposing query_graph, get_node, get_neighbors, god_nodes etc. so other agents can query the graph live.

nodes.label: Token Reduction Benchmark
nodes.rationale: Only run above 5,000 words — for small corpora the graph's value is structural clarity, not token compression.

nodes.label: Cross-Repo Graph Merge
nodes.rationale: Each subfolder/repo is extracted separately (CLI puts graphify-out inside the scanned path to avoid clobbering) then merge-graphs combines them; merged nodes carry a repo attribute for filtering by origin.

nodes.label: Post-Commit Auto-Rebuild Hook
nodes.rationale: Triggers once per commit with no background process; re-runs AST extraction on files changed in the commit, ignoring doc/image changes which need manual --update.

nodes.label: Native CLAUDE.md Integration
nodes.rationale: graphify claude install writes a '## graphify' section into the project CLAUDE.md so the graph is checked before answering codebase questions in every future session.

nodes.label: Whisper Video/Audio Transcription
nodes.rationale: Video/audio cannot be read directly; transcripts are produced first and then treated as doc files by semantic extraction.

nodes.label: Whisper Domain Hint Prompt
nodes.rationale: The host agent composes a one-sentence domain hint from god-node labels and passes it as Whisper's initial prompt — no separate API call needed.

nodes.label: Personal Information Triage System
nodes.rationale: Stuff enters the user's life faster than they can process it; the system externalizes the organizing-and-judgment layer without removing the user's agency over final decisions.

nodes.label: Source Data Immutability Constraint
nodes.rationale: The source Messages database (chat.db and sidecars) and Photos library are immutable with no exceptions; all access is copy-then-read and all mutation happens only in the local triage.db. This rule outranks every other instruction and every prompt.

nodes.label: Never Auto-Send Messages
nodes.rationale: Draft generation only, on explicit per-thread request; the user always copies and sends manually, preserving autonomy over communication.

nodes.label: API Context Minimization
nodes.rationale: Triage calls get contact profile + last 5 messages only, never full history — data minimization toward the Claude API.

nodes.label: Always Show Reasoning
nodes.rationale: Every triage/deletion-suggestion result carries a human-readable rationale string, not just a score — a transparency requirement, not a nice-to-have.

nodes.label: Retention Policy (14-Day Raw Content Deletion)
nodes.rationale: Raw message text and screenshot content are deleted from the local DB after the configured retention period; only derived signals persist past that window.

nodes.label: Dashboard-Only Deletion Review
nodes.rationale: No automatic deletion from the Photos library in v1 — the user acts on suggestions manually, keeping the human in the loop.

nodes.label: Vertical Slices Working Style
nodes.rationale: Get one thread or one screenshot flowing end-to-end before widening scope — surfaces integration problems early instead of at the end; plan before implementing, show evidence not assertions, checkpoint schemas before writing.

nodes.label: Local Triage DB (./data/triage.db)
nodes.rationale: The only mutable store in the system: SQLite holding derived signals only, downstream of the immutable sources.

nodes.label: Personal Triage Agent
nodes.rationale: Built to manage ADHD-related friction around responsiveness and information overload while demonstrating Socially Responsible Computing: how does an agent make judgment calls on someone's behalf without being useless (too conservative) or paternalistic (silently deciding for them)?

nodes.label: Privacy-by-Design Principles
nodes.rationale: Privacy-by-design, data minimization, user autonomy, and transparency govern both intake channels consistently; ambiguous decisions are flagged rather than silently chosen.

nodes.label: Contact Profiles (Derived Signals)
nodes.rationale: Per-thread median response latency, 90-day message frequency, and initiation ratio stored as derived signals only — never raw content.

nodes.label: Unanswered Thread Detection
nodes.rationale: Deterministic, no AI: last message is_from_me=0 and older than a configurable threshold (default 24h) — keeps the cheap filtering stage out of the LLM.

nodes.label: Per-Thread Triage Agent Call
nodes.rationale: One Claude API call per candidate thread with contact profile + last 5 messages only; structured output of urgency, reasoning, and suggest_nudge (model claude-sonnet-4-6).

nodes.label: Screenshot Vision Categorization
nodes.rationale: Each image goes through one Claude vision call producing category, extracted_text, description, tags, and a nullable expires_at; only derived metadata is stored, never full-res duplicates.

nodes.label: Screenshot Deletion Suggestions
nodes.rationale: Applies to screenshots/junk only — deliberately not extended to the general photo library (people/trips/memories) because that is too emotionally high-stakes for an automated suggestion pattern; never auto-deletes.

nodes.label: Accept/Reject Feedback Loop
nodes.rationale: Past accept/reject rates per category are pulled and included as context so Claude calibrates future suggestions — explicitly prompt-conditioning on aggregated history, NOT model fine-tuning.

nodes.label: No Draft-Generation Scope Limit
nodes.rationale: Drafting replies on someone's behalf reads as impersonal and disrespectful to the relationship; triage surfaces urgency and reasoning, the user writes their own replies.
